title: Lightly Fermented Greek Honey Loukoumades
description: A traditional Greek dessert featuring lightly fermented dough balls drizzled with honey and sprinkled with fines herbes, perfect for satisfying midnight cravings. This low-fat friendly treat is encrusted in a crunchy shell and filled with juicy sweetness.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, yeast, and salt.
Gradually add water and mix until a smooth dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it, and let it ferment in a warm place for 1 hour.
Preheat the oil in a deep frying pan to 375 degrees.
Using a spoon, drop the dough into the hot oil, making sure not to overcrowd the pan.
Fry the dough balls until they are golden brown and puffed up.
Drain the loukoumades on paper towels and drizzle with honey.
Sprinkle fines herbes and confectioners sugar over the top.
